task_description: Create a new Java project named TEST3 and run its default main program.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop centers on a desktop shortcut icon with the IntelliJ IDEA logo: a black square containing the letters "IJ" over overlapping bright red, orange, and blue geometric shapes. A semitransparent red X is placed directly over this icon. The label below reads "IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.3". In the full-screen view, this icon is on a Windows desktop among many application shortcuts and folders, positioned in the left-center area.
caption.action: Double-click the IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.3 desktop shortcut.
caption.expectation: IntelliJ IDEA should launch, opening its splash screen or main window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a dark IntelliJ IDEA welcome window with a rounded rectangular button labeled "新建项目" (New Project). A semitransparent red X is centered directly on this button, indicating the exact click target. This is a UI control in the top area of the welcome screen. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop, showing the welcome screen with recent projects listed in the center and nearby buttons including "打开" and "克隆仓库".
caption.action: Click the "新建项目" (New Project) button.
caption.expectation: The New Project setup screen or dialog should open, allowing configuration of a new IntelliJ project.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the IntelliJ IDEA 新建项目 (New Project) dialog’s language/template list, with a semitransparent red X centered on the row labeled “Java,” marked by a small orange coffee-cup icon. This is a clickable UI control in the dialog’s left-side options list. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop with the New Project window in front; “空项目” is currently highlighted below, and the right side shows project settings fields such as 名称 and 位置.
caption.action: Click the “Java” option in the New Project dialog’s language list.
caption.expectation: The Java project type should become selected, updating the dialog to show Java-specific project configuration options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the project name text field in IntelliJ IDEA’s New Project dialog, outlined in blue with the current text "untitled" inside. A semitransparent red X is centered directly over this editable text box, indicating the click target is the name input control. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop with the New Project dialog in front, Java selected in the language list, location set below the name field, and the Create and Cancel buttons at the bottom right.
caption.action: Click the project name text field containing "untitled".
caption.expectation: The name input field should gain focus and place the text cursor inside it, ready for editing or typing a new project name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the project name input box in IntelliJ IDEA with the text "untitled" highlighted by a semitransparent red drag path running horizontally across the word, indicating a text-selection drag from one side of the existing name to the other. In the full-screen view, the New Project dialog is open over the IntelliJ welcome window, Java is selected in the project type list, and the name field at the upper right is active above the location field and other project settings.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the existing "untitled" text in the project name field, drag along the shown path across the full word to select it, then release.
caption.expectation: The entire current project name should become selected, so it can be replaced immediately by typing a new name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the blue-outlined project name input box in IntelliJ IDEA’s New Project dialog, with the text insertion area containing the current name "untitled." A semitransparent red X is centered directly on this text field, so the click target is the editable name box itself, which is a UI control. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop in a RemoteApp window, the New Project dialog is in front, Java is selected in the project-type list, the location field is below the name field, and the Create and Cancel buttons are at the bottom right.
caption.action: Click the project name text field containing "untitled".
caption.expectation: The name field should become active and place the text cursor inside it, entering text-edit mode so the project name can be edited.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the project name text field outlined in blue in IntelliJ IDEA’s New Project dialog, with the current text beginning with "untitled" and a semitransparent red path running horizontally across the text box from left toward the right side, indicating a drag across the existing name to select the text. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open over the Windows desktop in the New Project window, Java is selected in the language list, the Name field is near the upper right above the Location field, and the Create and Cancel buttons are at the bottom right.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the existing text in the project name field, drag along the shown path across the name to the right end of the text box, then release.
caption.expectation: The current project name text should become selected, ready to be replaced with a new name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the active project name text field in IntelliJ IDEA’s New Project dialog. The field contains the partially entered lowercase text "tes", with the text cursor at the end, indicating new text is being entered into the selected name box and replacing the previously selected project name. A Chinese input candidate bar is visible below the field, and a red warning banner above notes only certain characters are allowed. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop with the New Project window in front, Java selected in the project type list, the Name field above the Location field on the right, and the Create and Cancel buttons at the bottom right.
caption.action: Type "TEST" into the active project name field, replacing the previously selected content.
caption.expectation: The project name field should update to show TEST as the new project name, with the text cursor remaining in the field.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the project name field area in IntelliJ IDEA with a Chinese input candidate bar displayed beneath it, listing numbered candidates such as "1 test", "2 特殊", "3 特色", "4 特俗", and more. A red X is visible over the candidate bar area, but the raw action is a keyboard action, so the intended operation is to confirm the current text entry rather than click. Above the field, a brown warning tooltip says the directory named "test" is not empty at this location. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA's New Project dialog is open on the Windows desktop, Java is selected in the project type list, the Name field contains "test", the Location field points to a path ending in "test", and the Create and Cancel buttons are at the bottom right.
caption.action: Press ENTER to confirm the current text input in the active project name field.
caption.expectation: The input method candidate bar should close, leaving the confirmed project name in the field so the dialog remains ready for the next step.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows IntelliJ IDEA's New Project dialog focused on the Name text field near the upper right. The field currently contains the lowercase text "test" with the text cursor at the end, and a brown warning tooltip above says the directory for test at this location is not empty. This indicates text is being entered into the project name field by appending to the existing content rather than replacing it. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open on the Windows desktop with Java selected in the project type list, the Location field below points to a path ending in a folder matching the project name, and the Create and Cancel buttons are visible at the bottom right.
caption.action: Type "3" into the active project name field, appending it to the existing text.
caption.expectation: The Name field should update from "test" to "test3", and the corresponding location preview should reflect the new project folder name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a blue rounded rectangular button labeled "创建(C)" with a semitransparent red X centered directly on it; this is a UI control in the bottom-right area of the dialog, next to a darker "取消" cancel button. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA's New Project dialog is open over the Windows desktop, Java is selected in the project type list, the project name field contains "test3", and the create button is enabled at the lower right.
caption.expectation: The New Project dialog should close and IntelliJ IDEA should begin creating and opening the new project.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blank dark area inside the code editor, immediately to the right of the string literal ending in a closing quotation mark and parenthesis on the line `System.out.printf("Hello and welcome");`. There is no button, icon, handle, or visible control under the X—just the editor background beside the code text. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open with the project `test3`, `Main.java` is selected in the project tree, the editor displays Java code with inline tutorial hints, and a lower line is highlighted in red with a breakpoint marker visible in the gutter. The click target is in the editor canvas area, not on the toolbar or a panel control.
caption.action: Click the blank region inside the code editor next to the `System.out.printf("Hello and welcome");` line.
caption.expectation: The editor should receive focus and place the text cursor near the clicked location, without opening any menu or changing tools.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the IntelliJ IDEA main toolbar's three-horizontal-lines button, the hamburger-style main menu icon, near the top-left of the window beside the IDE logo and the project name tab. This is a top-area UI control, not part of the editor canvas. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open with the project tree on the left, `Main.java` open in the editor, Java code visible with inline hints, and no menu currently expanded.
caption.action: Click the three-horizontal-lines main menu button in the top-left toolbar.
caption.expectation: The IDE's main menu or navigation panel should open from the top-left area, showing additional commands and options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a dark dropdown menu from IntelliJ IDEA with a semitransparent red X centered on the top menu item labeled "运行 'Main.java'(U)". The item has a green play triangle icon to its left, indicating a run command. In the full-screen view, IntelliJ IDEA is open with the project tree on the left, `Main.java` selected under `src`, Java code visible in the editor, and the Run menu expanded from the top menu bar.
caption.action: Click the top dropdown item labeled "运行 'Main.java'(U)" with the green play icon.
caption.expectation: The Run menu should close and IntelliJ IDEA should start running `Main.java`, opening or updating the Run tool window with program output.
